steps.title: Compare the consonant counts
steps.content: The passage says Tokelauan has eleven consonants and Olelo Hawaii has eight, while the global median is over twenty-two. The blank must describe Polynesian languages as having a relatively small array of consonant sounds.

steps.title: Choose the size description
steps.content: Choice A refers to music, not quantity. Choice B means unchanging, but the examples differ from each other. Choice C means restricted to a select group, not small in number. Modest means limited in size and fits the comparison. The answer is D.
